mission.title: cri-o: Add support for dual stack IPv6
mission.description:
We have to vendor the latest Kubernetes master as well as OCICNI to get
the latest support for dual-stack IPv6. This adds an `AdditionalIps`
field to the network status, which will be internally handled as a
simple slice of strings. This means that the pod annotations now can
contain multiple IPs as well. The same applied to the inspect HTTP API.

Relates to: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/73977
Current status: Under testing since the kubernetes PR has been merged. 

Closes #2554

resolution.summary:
**What type of PR is this?**
/kind api-change

/kind feature

**Implements**: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/pull/808
Included:
- [x] Types modification.
- [x] Conversions.
- [x] new conversion unit tests.
- [x] (updated existing unit tests to match the type changes).
- [x] node ipam controller that supports multi cidrs.
- [x] unit tests: node ipam mutli cidr.
- [x] azure cloud provider support for mutli routes per node.
- [x] unit tests: azure cloud provider support for mutli routes per node.
- [x] kubenet support for dual-stack.
- [x] revise host networking for dual-stack (v1.16).
- [x] unit tests: kubenet support for dual-stack.
- [x] e2e tests (as a stand alone PR referenced below).

```release-note
To configure controller manager to use ipv6dual stack:
use --cluster-cidr="<cidr1>,<cidr2>".

Notes:
 
1. Only the first two cidrs are used (soft limits for Alpha, might be lifted later on). 
2. Only the "RangeAllocator" (default) is allowed as a value for --cidr-allocator-type . Cloud allocators are not compatible with ipv6dualstack 
```

CC @lachie83 @thockin

### What is in the box? (alpha status)
- Node ipam controller now supports multi-cidr per node (node.Spec.PodCIDRs).
- Pod.PodStatus.PodIPs supports multiple IPs.
- Route Controller now supports creating routes `per node's cidr`.
- kubenet now supports dualstack. 
- Azure support `Route() interface` implementation for dualstack.
- Known issue: kubenet will always force reporting ipv4, ipv6 (irrespective of
steps: Only the first two cidrs are used (soft limits for Alpha, might be lifted later on).
Only the "RangeAllocator" (default) is allowed as a value for --cidr-allocator-type . Cloud allocators are not compatible with ipv6dualstack

CC @lachie83 @thockin ### What is in the box? (alpha status) - Node ipam controller now supports multi-cidr per node (node.Spec.PodCIDRs). - Pod.PodStatus.PodIPs supports multiple IPs. - Route Controller now supports creating routes `per node's cidr`. - kubenet now supports dualstack. - Azure support `Route() interface` implementation for dualstack. - Known issue: kubenet will always force reporting ipv4, ipv6 (irrespective of podCIDRs order). - updates to CRI to support reporting multiple IPs per `PodSandBox`. ### Known Issues - Cluster ipv6 CIDRS mask bigger than `24` will fail - ipv6 cidr assignment is using the default ipv4 cidr `/24` (Future: add controls over v6 cidr size) - kubenet forces `v4,v6` reporting of IPs, users who must`v6,v4` as `--cluster-cidr` - Masquerading is not done by kubenet. Users will have to use `ip-masq-agent` to perform masquerading correctly for ipv6.
